question: Who is the new Chief Minister of Bihar?
detail:
- BJP leader Samrat Choudhary has taken the oath as the 24th Chief Minister of Bihar.
- He has become the first BJP Chief Minister of Bihar.
- He is an MLA from the Tarapur assembly seat.
- Governor Syed Ata Hasnain administered the oath of office to Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary.
- He has replaced Nitish Kumar.
- Nitish Kumar is the longest-serving Chief Minister of Bihar in the 21st century.
- Nitish Kumar was elected to the Rajya Sabha from Bihar in March.
- He took the oath as Chief Minister a total of 10 times from 2000 to 2026.
- Two senior JDU leaders, Vijay Kumar Choudhary and Bijendra Prasad Yadav, have taken the oath as the new Deputy Chief Ministers.

**Bihar**
- Capital - Patna
- Governor - Syed Ata Hasnain
- CM - Samrat Choudhary
- Assembly Seats - 243
- Lok Sabha Seats - 40
- Rajya Sabha Seats - 16

question: The 7th edition of the joint military exercise 'Dustlick' was held between India and which country?
detail:
- The 7th edition of the India-Uzbekistan Joint Military Exercise 'DUSTLIK' was held from 12-25 April 2026.
- This year, the exercise was conducted at the Gurumsaray Field Training Area in Namangan, Uzbekistan.
- The main focus of this exercise is to practice Joint Operations and Tactical Drills to neutralize Unlawful Armed Groups in semi-mountainous terrain.

**What Is Exercise DUSTLIK?**
- It is an Annual Bilateral Military Drill between India and Uzbekistan.
- Its 6th edition was held in Aundh, Pune (India) in April 2025.
- Started - 2019

question: Which Indian player has been named the 'Wisden Leading Women's Cricketer in the World'?
detail:
- The 163rd edition of the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack has been announced.
- India's Deepti Sharma has received the 'Leading Women's Player in the World' award.
- Australia's fast bowler Mitchell Starc has received the honor of Leading Men's Cricketer in the World.
- India's Abhishek Sharma has been selected as the 'Leading T20 Player in the World'.
- Four Indians are included in Wisden's Five Cricketers of the Year - Shubman Gill, Ravindra Jadeja, Rishabh Pant, and Mohammed Siraj (the 5th player is England's Haseeb Hameed).
- Indian Captain Shubman Gill has also been honored with the 'Wisden Trophy' for his outstanding individual performance.

**What is ‘Wisden Cricketers’ Almanack?**
- It is a cricket reference book published annually in the United Kingdom.
- It is known as the "Bible of Cricket".
- It was started by John Wisden in 1864.

question: Recently, 'Shekha Jheel Bird Sanctuary' has been designated as India's 99th Ramsar Site. In which state is it located?
detail:
- The Shekha Jheel Bird Sanctuary in Aligarh (UP) has been granted Ramsar Site status. It has become India's 99th Ramsar Site.
- It is a fresh water lake that serves as an important stopover for migratory birds of the Central Asian Flyway.

**Key Points & Data**
- Location - This lake is located near the Aligarh-Khurja link canal.
- Species - Over 170 species of birds are found here.
- Total Ramsar Sites in India – 99
- Note - Patna Bird Sanctuary in UP and Chhari-Dhand in Kutch, Gujarat, were declared Ramsar Sites in February 2026.

question: According to India's first 'State of India's Bats Report (2024-25)' released in April 2026, which state has the highest diversity of bats?
detail:
- The Nature Conservation Foundation (NCF) and Bat Conservation International (BCI) have released India's first 'State of India’s Bats Report (2024-25)'.
- Its main objective is to gather accurate information regarding the number of bat species so that sound and correct environmental policies can be formulated.
- There are 135 species of bats in India, of which 16 are endemic and 7 are critically endangered.
- States with the Most Diversity - West Bengal (68 species) and Meghalaya (66 species) are at the top.

question: Under which Article has the Supreme Court considered Road Safety as a part of the 'Right to Life' of citizens?
detail:
- A Supreme Court bench delivered this landmark judgment while hearing a Public Interest Litigation (PIL).
- The Court clarified that the Right to Safe Roads is now an integral part of the 'Right to Life' under Article 21.
- This decision was taken considering the threat to citizens' lives due to increasing road accidents and poor infrastructure in India.

question: What is India's rank in terms of patent filing globally in FY 2025-26?
detail:
- India has become the world's 6th largest country in terms of patent filing in the Financial Year 2025-26.
- It has registered an impressive growth of 30.2% compared to the last financial year.
- (There has been a 146% increase over the last 5 years).
- A record 1,43,729 patents have been filed in India in the Financial Year 2025-26.
- Approximately 69% of the patents were filed by domestic applicants.

**Top states in India**
- Tamil Nadu
- Karnataka
- Maharashtra

**Top 5 Countries**
- China
- USA
- Japan
- South Korea
- Germany

**What Is a Patent?**
- A patent is a legal right granted to an inventor for a new invention.

question: Who has become the first Indian woman to win the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament 2026?
detail:
- Indian Grandmaster R. Vaishali has won the 'FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament 2026' held in Cyprus.
- She has become the first Indian to win this prestigious tournament.
- She secured the victory by defeating Russia's Kateryna Lagno in the final round.
- With this win, she has qualified for the Women's World Chess Championship.
- The winner of the Candidates challenges the World Champion.
- She will challenge the defending champion, Ju Wenjun of China.
- Vaishali will be only the second Indian woman to fight for the World Championship after Koneru Humpy (2011).
- Viswanathan Anand (1995) - Won and challenged Garry Kasparov.
- Viswanathan Anand (2014) - Played the final against Carlsen.
- D. Gukesh (2024) - Youngest Candidates winner, became World Champion.
- R. Vaishali (2026) - First Indian woman to win the Candidates.

**What is the "FIDE Candidates Tournament"?**
- It is the second biggest and most important tournament in the chess world.
- It is the qualifier tournament for the World Chess Championship.
- The player who wins this tournament gets the opportunity to play a title match against the World Chess Champion.
- It is organized by FIDE (International Chess Federation).
- Note - Open (Male) Candidates 2026 Winner - Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan (He will now challenge India's World Champion D. Gukesh in the final).

question: Who has won the title of 61st Femina Miss India World 2026?
detail:
- Sadhvi Satish Sail from Goa has won the title of the 61st Femina Miss India Pageant.
- The grand finale was held at the Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology (KIIT) in Bhubaneswar (Odisha).
- She has become the first woman from the state of Goa to win this prestigious crown.
- She was crowned by the former winner, Nikita Porwal.
- Now, Sadhvi Satish Sail will represent India in the upcoming Miss World 2027 competition to be held in Tanzania.

**What Is Femina Miss India?**
- It is a National Beauty Pageant.
- The winner gets the opportunity to represent India in international pageants like Miss World.
- It is organized by 'The Times Group'.
- Nikita Porwal from Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, is the winner of Femina Miss India World 2024.
- Miss World 2026 - Nikita Porwal is representing India at the 75th Miss World pageant to be held in Vietnam this year (2026). This competition is scheduled between August-September 2026.

question: Which statement is correct regarding the 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize?
options: The Prize Money is $200,000 per Winner
It is known as the "Green Nobel"
For the first time in history, all 6 winners are women
detail:
- The Goldman Environmental Prize 2026 has been announced, in which 6 female activists from 6 geographic regions of the world have been selected.
- Each winner is awarded \$200,000 (approximately Rs. 1.6 crores).
- For the first time since the inception of the prize in 1989, all awards were given to women.

**Winners**
- Iroro Tanshi | Nigeria (Africa) | Saved a sanctuary by stopping forest fires.
- Borim Kim | South Korea (Asia) | Forced the government to create strict climate targets.
- Sarah Finch | UK (Europe) | Won a long legal battle against fossil fuels and oil drilling.
- Theonila Roka | Papua New Guinea (Island country) | Pollution Control.
- Alanna Akak Hurley | Americas (North America) | Saved 'Salmon Fishes' and the ecosystem in Alaska.
- Yuvelis Morales | Colombia (South America) | Assisted in preventing pollution of the Magdalena River in Colombia.

**Goldman Environmental Prize**
- Established - 1989
- It is called the "Green Nobel".
- Regions - It is given to grassroots activists from 6 regions (Africa, Asia, Europe, Islands & Island Nations, North America, South & Central America).
- Indian Winners - Medha Patkar (1992), Prafulla Samantara (2017), Alok Shukla (2024).

question: UNESCO has officially named which Moroccan city as the 'World Book Capital' for the year 2026?
detail:
- UNESCO has declared Rabat as the World Book Capital for the year 2026. This announcement was made on the occasion of April 23 (World Book and Copyright Day).
- This honor has been bestowed upon the city for its efforts to enhance literacy, develop the book industry, and promote a knowledge economy.

**World Book Capital**
- It is an initiative by UNESCO.
- This program started in 2001 with Madrid.
- Every year, a city is declared as the “World Book Capital” for 1 year.
- Objective – To promote a Reading Culture, the Publishing Industry, and literacy.
- NOTE - 2025 World Book Capital - Rio de Janeiro (Brazil).

question: Dr. Vijaypat Singhania, who passed away recently, was awarded the 'Padma Bhushan' award in which year?
detail:
- Former Chairman of the Raymond Group, Vijaypat Singhania, passed away in Mumbai at the age of 87.
- The Indian government honored him with the 'Padma Bhushan', the country's third-highest civilian award, in the year 2006 for his exceptional contribution to the fields of trade, industry, and aviation.

**Raymond Legacy**
- He played a crucial role in making Raymond one of the largest apparel brands in India.

**Autobiography**
- 'An Incomplete Life' (This book was widely discussed due to the legal disputes between him and his son, Gautam Singhania).

**Raymond Group**
- Founded - 1925.
- Headquarters - Mumbai, Maharashtra.
- Current Chairman - Gautam Singhania.

question: Which Indian author has been honored with the National Book Critics Circle Award 2025 (Autobiography)?
detail:
- Indian writer Arundhati Roy has been honored with the prestigious National Book Critics Circle (NBCC) Award 2025 of the USA (in the Autobiography category).
- For her memoir "Mother Mary Comes to Me".
- This book is based on the relationship between her and her mother (Mary Roy) and the struggles of their lives.
- At the same award ceremony, South Korean Nobel winner Han Kang won the NBCC Award in the Fiction category for her novel "We Do Not Part".
- Previously, Arundhati Roy received the Booker Prize in 1997 for her book “The God of Small Things”.

**NBCC (National Book Critics Circle) Award**
- It is a major literary award in the USA.
- It is given annually to the best books published in English across 6 categories (Fiction, Nonfiction, Biography, Autobiography, Poetry, and Criticism).

question: Who has been appointed as the Brand Ambassador for 'Census 2027' by the Government of India?
detail:
- Sudarsan Pattnaik has been appointed as the Brand Ambassador for 'Census 2027' by the Indian government.
- This appointment has been made by the Ministry of Home Affairs.
- Sudarsan Pattnaik is an internationally renowned sand artist.
- He will raise awareness among people to participate in this census through his sand art.

**Sudarsan Pattnaik**
- Born - 15 April 1977 (Age 48) Puri, Odisha, India
- Occupation - Sand artist
- Awards - Padma Shri (2014)
- People’s Choice Award at the Sand Sculpting World Cup 2014.
- First Indian to win the Italian Sand Art Award in 2019.
- First Indian to win the Fred Darrington Sand Master Award 2025 in the UK.

question: Who was awarded the Lata Deenanath Mangeshkar Award for the year 2026?
detail:
- Nobel Peace Prize Winner Kailash Satyarthi was honored with the 'Lata Deenanath Mangeshkar Award 2026'.
- This honor was presented on April 24, 2026, at the Ganesh Kala Krida Manch in Pune on the 84th death anniversary of Master Deenanath Mangeshkar.

**Other Major Winners**
- Cinema/Theatre - Raveena Tandon, Subodh Bhave, Prajakta Mali.
- Music - Mahesh Kale.
- Literature - Achyut Godbole.
- Journalism - Sushil Kulkarni.
- Social Service - Anand Bhagwat.
- Mohan Wagh Award (Best Theatre Production) - Given to the play 'Bhumika'.

**What is the Lata Deenanath Mangeshkar Award?**
- This award was established in 2022 in memory of Bharat Ratna Lata Mangeshkar.
- It is given annually to only one individual who has made path-breaking contributions to the nation, society, and art.
- The first recipient of this award was PM Narendra Modi.

**Kailash Satyarthi**
- He is a renowned Indian child rights activist.
- Born - 11 January 1954
- Place of Birth - Vidisha, Madhya Pradesh

**Work Contribution**
- He founded the "Bachpan Bachao Andolan" in 1980.
- He has saved thousands of children from child labor and trafficking.
- He ran a global campaign for the Right to Education and Child Rights.
- Book - “Karuna: The Power of Compassion”
- Year 2014 - Nobel Peace Prize.

question: 'World Heritage Day' is celebrated every year on which date?
detail:
- International Day for Monuments and Sites is celebrated every year on April 18, which is also known as World Heritage Day.
- Its objective is to raise awareness about the world's cultural heritage and to focus on its safety and protection.
- This day was proposed by ICOMOS (International Council on Monuments and Sites) in 1982.
- UNESCO officially approved it in 1983, and it is now celebrated every year on April 18.
- Theme 2026 - “Emergency Response for Living Heritage in contexts of Conflicts and Disasters”.

question: To what record level has India's total exports reached in the financial year 2025-26?
detail:
- India's total exports (combining both Goods and Services) have reached a record level of \$860 Billion in the financial year 2025-26.
- This was announced by the Union Commerce and Industry Minister, Piyush Goyal.
- This is the highest-ever export record to date.
- In the last financial year (2024-25), this figure was USD 825 Billion.

question: Who has become India's 95th Chess Grandmaster in April 2026?
detail:
- 22-year-old chess player Aronyak Ghosh from Kolkata, West Bengal, has become India's 95th Chess Grandmaster.

**Note**
- 93rd GM - Aarav Dengla.
- 94th GM - Mayank Chakraborty.
- First GM of India - Viswanathan Anand (1988).
- First Female GM of India - Koneru Humpy.

question: Chandrikapersad Santokhi, of Indian origin, recently passed away. He was the former President of which country?
detail:
- Former President of Suriname, Chandrikapersad Santokhi, has passed away at the age of 67.
- He served as the President of Suriname from 2020 to 2025.
- He was also the chairman of the Progressive Reform Party.
- Being of Indian origin (Bihar), he took his oath of office in Sanskrit in 2020.
- Chandrikapersad Santokhi was honored with the “Pravasi Bharatiya Samman” in the year 2021.

question: Which became the first country in Asia to ban social media for children under 16 years of age?
detail:
- The Indonesian Government has imposed a ban on children under 16 years of age from holding social media accounts.
- This rule will apply to high-risk platforms such as YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, and Facebook. Its main aim is to protect children from harmful online content, cyberbullying, and digital addiction.

**Objectives Of The Scheme/Event**
- To protect children from things like pornography, online fraud, and violence.
- To give parents more control and monitoring over their children's online activities.
- To make social media companies legally responsible for the age verification process.
- To protect the mental health of children in situations like a digital emergency.

**Note**
- In December 2025, Australia also became the first country in the world to implement a ban on social media for children under the age of 16.

question: Which Indian city has recently been included in the inaugural list of the UN "20 Cities Towards Zero Waste" initiative?
detail:
- The coastal city of Varkala in Kerala has been included in the inaugural list of the United Nations' "20 Cities Towards Zero Waste" initiative.
- This is a major achievement for India.
- This initiative is primarily led by the UN Secretary-General’s Advisory Board on Zero Waste.
- It is supported by the UNEP (United Nations Environment Programme).
- Its objective is to recognize and honor those 20 cities worldwide that have demonstrated outstanding innovation and leadership in promoting waste reduction and a circular economy.
- The list of these 20 cities was recently released on the occasion of the 'International Day of Zero Waste' (March 30, 2026).

question: Who became the first woman selected for a Moon flyby mission?
detail:
- Christina Koch is the first woman selected for a Moon Flyby Mission.
- This is the second stage of NASA's Artemis II Program. Its main objective is to return humans to the vicinity of the moon after 50 years.
- Meaning of Flyby - The spacecraft will not land on the moon but will return to Earth after orbiting it (lunar orbit).

**Record and role of Christina Koch**
- Christina Koch is the first female astronaut included in this mission who will go near the moon.
- Role - Mission Specialist.
- Records - Longest spaceflight by a woman (328 days).

**Artemis I (2022)**
- Launch - November 2022
- Type - Uncrewed (no humans)
- Objective:
  - To test the Orion Spacecraft.
  - To check the performance of the SLS Rocket (the world's most powerful rocket).
  - To test the capability of safely orbiting the moon and returning.
- This was completely a “Trial Run”.

**Artemis II (2025–26)**
- Launch - April 1, 2026
- Location - Kennedy Space Center, Florida
- Type - Crewed (4 astronauts sent)

question: Legendary singer Asha Bhosle passed away on April 12, 2026. In which year was she honored with the 'Padma Vibhushan’ award?

question: Who has been awarded the ‘WWF Machchli National Award 2026’?
detail:
- Forest Guard Anita Chaudhary of Rajasthan was honored with the WWF Machchli National Award 2026.
- She was given this honor for wildlife conservation, preventing poaching, and successfully rescuing more than 500 wild animals (especially crocodiles).
- Anita Chaudhary is posted in the Shergarh Wildlife Sanctuary of Rajasthan.

**What is the "WWF Machchli Award"?**
- This award is given by the World-Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) – India.
- It is named after Machchli (the famous tigress of Ranthambore).

**WWF (World Wide Fund for Nature)**
- Founded - 1961
- Headquarter - Gland, Switzerland

question: Which Indian actor has been included in the recently released 'TIME 100 Most Influential People 2026' list?
detail:
- TIME Magazine has released the prestigious list of the 100 Most Influential People in the world for the year 2026.
- Three prominent Indians have been included in this global list - Sundar Pichai (Google CEO), Vikas Khanna (Famous Chef), and Ranbir Kapoor (Actor).
- Sundar Pichai - CEO of Google and Alphabet.
- Vikas Khanna - He is a famous Indian chef. He has been included for making Indian cuisine popular worldwide and for his generosity (especially his restaurant 'Bungalow').
- Ranbir Kapoor - He is the only Indian actor to be included in this year's list.

**What Is the TIME 100 List?**
- It is a list released annually by the American news magazine Time Magazine.
- It recognizes 100 individuals who have had the most profound impact on global politics, business, arts, science, and society.

**TIME Magazine**
- Founded - 1923
- Headquarter - New York, USA

question: Who won the "World Sportsman of the Year" at the 2026 Laureus World Sports Awards?
detail:

- These awards are organized to honor the top athletes, teams, and sports in the sporting world.
- This award is called the "Oscars Of Sports".
- Spanish tennis star Carlos Alcaraz was named 'World Sportsman of the Year'.
- In the women's category, tennis player Aryna Sabalenka of Belarus achieved the prestigious honor of 'World Sportswoman of the Year'.

**Laureus World Sports Awards**
- The Laureus World Sports Awards is a prestigious annual award.
- It is presented to recognize outstanding performance in sports.
- It is organized by the Laureus Sports Foundation.
- Start - 1999

question: Where is India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park being established?
detail:
- India's first Petroglyph Conservation Park is being established in Leh, Ladakh.
- This is to protect thousands-of-years-old rock carvings (petroglyphs).
- The petroglyphs in Ladakh date back to the Neolithic period (approximately 10,000 BC - 2,000 BC), which were endangered due to development activities and climate change.
- The main objective is to protect ancient rock carvings from the pressure of infrastructure development and tourism.
- There are about 400 petroglyph sites in Ladakh, where inscriptions in ancient languages like Chinese, Arabic, and Sanskrit are also found.

**What are Petroglyphs?**
- These are prehistoric drawings or symbols.
- They are carved or engraved directly onto rock surfaces.
- The petroglyphs found in Ladakh depict animals (like the snow leopard and ibex), hunting scenes, and Buddhist symbols from later periods.

question: India's defence exports have reached an all- time high of how many rupees in the financial year 2025-26?
detail:
- In the Financial Year 2025-26, India's defence export records reached a historic high of ₹38,424 crore.
- This is a massive increase of 62.66% compared to the previous year (₹23,622 crore).
- Currently, India is exporting equipment like missile systems (e.g., BrahMos), radars, and armored vehicles to more than 80 countries (such as the US, France, Armenia).
- The number of registered defence exporters in the country has increased from 128 to 145.

question: India's most expensive painting 'Yashoda and Krishna' has sold for ₹167.20 crore, who made it?
detail:
- Raja Ravi Varma's painting 'Yashoda and Krishna' was auctioned for ₹167.20 crore in Mumbai.
- This has become India's most expensive painting.
- This is a famous painting by Raja Ravi Varma from the 1890s.
- 'Yashoda and Krishna' was auctioned by the Mumbai-based auction house Saffronart for ₹167.2 crore (\$17.9 million).
- This painting was purchased by Cyrus S. Poonawalla, Chairman of the Serum Institute of India.
- This sale surpassed the old record (₹118 crore) of M.F. Husain's painting 'Untitled (Gram Yatra)'.

**Raja Ravi Varma**
- Raja Ravi Varma was one of India's greatest painters.
- He is considered the pioneer of modern Indian art.
- Born - April 29, 1848
- Location - Kilimanoor, Kerala
- His most famous paintings include 'Shakuntala' and 'Arjuna and Subhadra'.

question: Who is the author of the book “We, the People of India: Decoding a Nation’s Symbols”?
detail:
- T.M. Krishna is the author of the book “We, the People of India: Decoding a Nation’s Symbols”.
- This book tells the story of the meaning and evolution of India's national symbols (like the National Anthem, Flag, Emblem).

**T. M. Krishna**
- Born - January 22, 1976, Chennai
- Profession - Carnatic classical music singer, writer, social activist.
- Ramon Magsaysay Award (2016) – For social work and art.

question: Which country topped the doping violation list released by Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU)?
detail:
- In the report of the Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU), India ranked first with 148 suspended athletes.
- Kenya – 146 athletes (2nd) and Russia – 66 athletes (3rd).
- This list is based on data up to April 1, 2026, and it includes violations such as tampering, evading tests, and trafficking in addition to doping.

**What Is the Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU)?**
- AIU is an independent anti-doping body.
- It was established by World Athletics.
- It enforces doping tests and suspensions for international athletes and their support staff.

question: ‘Rongali Bihu’ festival marks the New Year and arrival of spring in which state?
detail:
- The ‘Rongali Bihu’ festival was celebrated in Assam starting from April 5th.
- This festival marks the arrival of the Assamese New Year and the Spring Season.
- It is also called Bohag Bihu and is celebrated in April.
- Events like Bihu Dance (Rongili), Quiz Competition, and Art Event (Kalakriti) were organized during the program.
- Bihu is the biggest and most famous festival of Assam.
- It is mainly associated with agriculture (farming) and is celebrated three times a year.
- During this festival, people celebrate nature, the harvest, and the new year.

**There are three types of Bihu**
- Rongali Bihu (Baisakh/April) – Celebration of the new year and the beginning of farming.
- Bhogali Bihu (January) – Celebration of the harvest and feasting.
- Kongali Bihu (October) – A quiet/prayerful festival in the midst of the farming cycle.

question: According to Skytrax World Airport Awards 2026, which is the top-ranked airport in India?
detail:
- In the Skytrax World Airport Awards 2026, 5 Indian Airports were included in the Global Top 100.
- It ranks the airports of the world based on passenger feedback.
- Indira Gandhi International Airport (Delhi) secured the top position in India with the 28th rank.
- Singapore's Changi Airport has become the world's Best Airport for a record 14th time.

**Top Performer in India**
- Delhi – Indira Gandhi International Airport - 28th
- Bengaluru – Kempegowda International Airport - 41st
- Hyderabad – Rajiv Gandhi International Airport - 43rd
- Goa – Manohar International Airport - 64th
- Mumbai – Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport - 66th

**Top 3 Airports**
- 1st Rank - Singapore Changi Airport (Singapore)
- 2nd Rank - Incheon International Airport (South Korea)
- 3rd Rank - Tokyo Haneda Airport (Japan)

**What is Skytrax**
- It is an International Rating Agency.
- It ranks the world's airports based on passenger feedback.
- It is considered similar to the 'Oscars of the Aviation Industry'.
- Start - 1999

question: Which state has topped in drone pilot training under the ‘Namo Drone Didi Yojana’?
detail:
- Karnataka achieved the top position in Drone Pilot Training under the Namo Drone Didi Yojana.
- Karnataka has provided Drone Pilot Training to 145 Women SHG Members.

**Other States**
- Uttar Pradesh – 128
- Andhra Pradesh – 108
- Aim - To provide agricultural services by giving drones to Women's Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and to increase their income.

**‘Namo Drone Didi Yojana’**
- It is an initiative of the Indian government.
- It empowers Women's Self-Help Groups (SHGs) by providing them with drone technology for agricultural purposes.
- Under this, drones are being provided to 15,000 women SHGs between 2024-25 and 2025-26.
- The goal is for them to earn an additional annual income of ₹1 lakh by providing fertilizer/pesticide spraying services in farming.
- Selected women are given 15 days of drone flying and technical training (Remote Pilot Certificate).
- It is a Central Sector Scheme.
- Implementation period - 2023-24 to 2025-26.
- Target - To provide agricultural drones to 15,000 Women SHGs.
- Total Budget - ₹1,261 crore.

question: Which film was awarded 'Best Film' at the Chetak Screen Awards 2026?
detail:
- The film 'Homebound', directed by Neeraj Ghaywan, was awarded the prestigious 'Best Film' award at the Chetak Screen Awards 2026.
- The Chetak Screen Awards were organized in Mumbai, making a comeback with a live broadcast after 20 years.
- This event was hosted by Alia Bhatt, Sunil Grover, and Zakir Khan.

**Data/Ranking (Winners)**
- Best Actor (Male) - Ranveer Singh – Film “Dhurandhar”
- Best Actor (Female) - Yami Gautam – Film “Haq”
- Best Director - Aditya Dhar – “Dhurandhar”
- Dominance - “Dhurandhar” set a record by winning a total of 14 awards.

**Organization**
- These awards are organized by The Indian Express Group.

**Screen Awards**
- First organized - 1995
- Organizer - The Indian Express Group
- Categories - 31 (Both Theatre + OTT)

question: Who has become the new President of Vietnam?
detail:
- To Lam has been elected as the new President of Vietnam.
- To Lam was elected as President for the 2026-2031 term.
- He is the Chief of the Communist Party.
- He will hold both the Communist Party Chief and President positions.
- He received 99% of the vote.
- To Lam took over the post of President, replacing Luong Cuong.

question: How much wind power capacity did India add in FY 2025-26, setting an all-time annual record?
detail:
- India has set a new record by adding 6.05 GW of Wind Energy Capacity in the Financial Year 2024-25.
- This is higher than the previous record (5.5 GW in 2016-17) and is the highest annual addition to date.
- India is in the 4th position globally in Wind Energy Installed Capacity (after China, USA, and Germany).
- The total installed Wind Energy Capacity has now crossed 49 GW.
- Main Objective - Wind Energy plays an important role in the target of achieving 500 GW of Non-fossil Fuel Capacity by 2030.

**Ministry Of New And Renewable Energy (MNRE)**
- Headquarter - New Delhi
- Current Minister - Pralhad Joshi

**Wind Energy**
- State with the highest capacity - Gujarat (Tamil Nadu is in the second position)

question: Who became the first Indian woman to win the title of 'Mrs International World Classic 2026’?
detail:
- Mrs. Geetanjali Om Parkash received the crown of Mrs International World Classic 2026.
- She has become the first Indian woman to win this prestigious global title.
- It was organized in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.
- Geetanjali Om Prakash (59 Years old) is the Managing Director of Dr. Om Parkash Group of Eye Institutes located in Amritsar, Punjab.

**Mrs International World Classic 2026**
- It is an International Beauty Pageant category,
- which is usually organized for married women (Mrs Category).
- It is organized by the International Pageant Organization (the institution that runs the Mrs International/World Classic category).
- International Pageant Organizations are institutions that organize beauty pageants worldwide (like Miss, Mrs, Teen, etc.).
- It is an annually held pageant.

question: According to the Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA) report, which was the most polluted city in India in March 2026?
detail:
- The Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA) has released a new report.
- According to this report, Gurugram in Haryana became the most polluted city in India in March 2026.
- According to this report, Gurugram's monthly average PM2.5 level was recorded at 116 μg/m3 in March.
- This is almost double India's national safe standard (60 μg/m3) and much higher than the WHO standard (15 μg/m3).
- Four cities from Haryana are included in India's top 10 most polluted cities for the month of March.
- These include the names of Gurugram, Bahadurgarh, Faridabad, and Manesar.
- In the financial year 2025-26, Ghaziabad in Uttar Pradesh (with 215 μg/m3 PM10) was the most polluted city in the country, followed by Delhi and Noida.

| Rank | City | State |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Gurugram | Haryana |
| 2 | Bahadurgarh | Haryana |
| 3 | Faridabad | Haryana |

question: Which new initiative has been launched by the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) to plant medicinal trees along National Highways?
detail:
- The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has launched an initiative to plant medicinal trees under the name 'Arogya Van' on vacant lands along the National Highways.
- About 36 medicinal tree species have been selected for this work.
- These include Neem, Amla, Tamarind, Jamun, Lemon, Gular, and Maulsari, which will be planted according to the local climate.
- The main objective of this scheme is to promote ancient medical practices like Ayurveda and to create biodiversity for birds and small animals.
- Under Phase-1, 17 land parcels across 11 states (MP, Haryana, DELHI-NCR, Andhra Pradesh, Gujarat, Karnataka, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Rajasthan, Maharashtra, and Chhattisgarh) have been selected.
- Their total area is 62.8 hectares, and approximately 67,462 medicinal trees will be planted here.

**National Highways Authority Of India (NHAI)**
- Formation Year - 1988
- Headquarter - New Delhi
- Ministry - Ministry Of Road Transport And Highways

question: Recently, which state government has declared cancer as a 'Notifiable Disease’?
detail:
- The Telangana government has officially declared cancer as a 'Notifiable Disease'.
- It is now mandatory for all government and private hospitals to report cases.
- Under the new rules, it will be mandatory for all official and private hospitals and labs to report new cancer cases on the State Portal within 1 month of detection.

question: When is "World Homeopathy Day" observed?
detail:
- "World Homeopathy Day" is observed every year on April 10th.
- This day is celebrated every year on the birth anniversary of Dr. Christian Friedrich Samuel Hahnemann, who is regarded as the father of Homeopathy.
- Samuel Hahnemann was a German physician who discovered Homeopathy.
- This day was first established in 2005 by the World Homeopathy Awareness Organization (WHAO), after which it has been celebrated annually.
- 2026 Theme - “Homoeopathy for Sustainable Health”.

question: According to the 'Renewable Energy Statistics 2026', what is India's rank in the world in terms of renewable energy installed capacity?
detail:
- The International Renewable Energy Agency has released the 'Renewable Energy Statistics 2026 Report'.
- India has surpassed Brazil to secure the 3rd position in the World Ranking in Renewable Energy Capacity.

**Global Ranking**
- 1st - China - 2258.02 GW.
- 2nd - USA - 467.92 GW.
- 3rd - India - 250.52 GW.

**Data (Up to March 2026)**
- India's Total Non-fossil Capacity - 283.46 GW.
- Out of this, Renewable Energy - 274.68 GW.
- A record 55.29 GW capacity was added in FY 2025-26.

**International Renewable Energy Agency**
- Headquarter - Abu Dhabi
- Establishment - 2009

question: Who has been awarded the '9th Prof. M.S. Swaminathan Award (2024–25)’?
detail:
- Dr. Ch. Srinivasa Rao has been awarded the '9th Prof. M.S. Swaminathan Award (2024–25)'.
- Dr. Ch. Srinivasa Rao is the current Director & Vice-Chancellor of the Indian Agricultural Research Institute.
- He was given this award in Hyderabad for his groundbreaking work in Climate-Resilient Agriculture and Natural Resource Management.
- This award includes a Gold Medal, a citation, and a cash prize of ₹2 Lakh.

**What is the M.S. Swaminathan Award?**
- It is a major honor in the Indian Agriculture Sector.
- It was established in 2004-05 by the Retired ICAR Employees Association (RICAREA) and Nuziveedu Seeds Limited (NSL) to honor agricultural scientists for their lifetime achievement and contribution to the food security of the country.

question: Padma Shri awardee Bhagwandas Raikwar passed away at the age of 83. He was associated with which field?
detail:
- Madhya Pradesh's 'Bundeli Martial Arts' Guru Bhagwandas Raikwar has passed away at the age of 83.
- He was honored with the Padma Shri on Republic Day 2026 under the 'Unsung Heroes' category in the field of Sports (Traditional Martial Arts).
- He established the Chhatrasal Bundelkhand Akhada in Sagar (MP) in 1964 with his limited resources.
- He was a master of weapon-based combat, proficient in using the stick (lathi), sword, spear, shield, and trident.

question: Which AI chatbot has been launched by the CBI to tackle 'Digital Arrest' frauds and verify notices?
detail:
- The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has launched an AI chatbot named ‘ABHAY’.
- The CBI has introduced this AI-powered chatbot to protect citizens from fake legal notices and online fraud.
- Starting May 1, 2026, all official notices from the CBI will have a special QR code. Citizens can scan this code using ABHAY to verify the genuineness of the notice.

question: Recently in the news, the 'RELOS' pact is related to 'Reciprocal Logistics Exchange' between which two countries?
detail:
- The RELOS (Reciprocal Exchange of Logistics Support) agreement between India and Russia has now been fully implemented.

**RELOS Agreement?**
- It is a Military Logistics Agreement between two countries.
- Through this agreement, the two countries can use each other's military facilities for refuelling, repairs, supplies, berthing (parking ships), and maintenance.
- This agreement was signed between the two countries on February 18th.

**How will India benefit from this?**
- India can access Russia's Military, Naval, and Air Bases at any time.
- India's influence in the Arctic and Pacific Oceans will also increase.
- Defense cooperation between India and Russia will grow significantly.

question: Garia Puja is primarily celebrated in which state?
detail:
- The Garia Puja festival was celebrated in Tripura.
- The Garia Puja festival is celebrated every year in the month of Boishakh (Tripuri/Bengali calendar). This time it was celebrated from April 21 to 27.
- This festival is celebrated for 7 days.
- During this festival, the Kokborok-speaking tribes and Halam tribes of Tripura worship their deity, Baba Garia.

**What is Kokborok?**
- Kokborok is the mother tongue of the Borok people and tribal communities in Tripura.
- A history of the Tripuri kings was written in Kokborok and the Koloma script.
- Kokborok was recognized as the official language of Tripura in 1979.
- This language is spoken by 23.97% of the population of Tripura.

question: Who has been appointed as the first woman Chief Justice of the Andhra Pradesh High Court?
detail:
- Justice Lisa Gill has been appointed as the new Chief Justice of the Andhra Pradesh High Court.
- She has become the first woman to head the Andhra Pradesh High Court.
- She has replaced Dhiraj Singh Thakur.
- Prior to this appointment, she was serving in the Punjab and Haryana High Court.

**Constitutional Basis of Appointments**
- These appointments are made under Article 217 and Article 222 of the Indian Constitution.

question: National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated on 24 April, the provision of Panchayat is in which schedule of the Indian Constitution?
detail:
- National Panchayati Raj Day is celebrated every year on April 24th.
- This year, the 16th National Panchayati Raj Day was celebrated.
- In 1882, Lord Ripon provided the framework for elected local boards — this is called the Magna Carta of local democracy in India.
- Panchayats are discussed in the Directive Principles under Article 40.
- Panchayati Raj was introduced under the Constitution (73rd Amendment) Act, 1992.
- This amendment added Part 9, which contains Articles 243 to 243(O), and added the 11th Schedule, which has 29 subjects related to Panchayats.

**History of Panchayat Raj Day**
- The first National Panchayati Raj Day was declared in 2010 by former Prime Minister Manmohan Singh.
- Since 2011, April 24th has been celebrated as National Panchayat Raj Day.

question: Which Indian botanist was awarded the WWF National Award 2026?
detail:
- Indian botanist N. Alim Yusuf (Kerala) was awarded the WWF National Award 2026.
- This honor was given at the National ECHO Youth Innovation Summit 2026 held in Hyderabad.
- This award honors those doing outstanding work in the field of Environmental Protection and saving nature.
- He was given this award for developing an AI-powered mobile app called 'NeophyteID'.
- This app is capable of identifying about 100 Invasive Plant Species in Kerala.
- He has also received a Seed Grant of Rs 1.5 Lakh from WWF to take this project forward.

**WWF (World Wide Fund for Nature)**
- Aim - Protection of Nature and Wildlife and keeping the environment safe.
- Founded - 1961
- Headquarter - Gland, Switzerland

question: Who has been elected as the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha for the third consecutive time?
detail:
- Harivansh Narayan Singh has been elected unopposed as the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha for the third consecutive time.
- His previous tenure ended on April 9, 2026.
- After which, President Droupadi Murmu nominated him again as a Nominated Member to the Upper House.
- The motion to make him Vice-chairman was moved by the Leader of the House, J. P. Nadda.
- Harivansh is the first Nominated Member to reach this position.
- He was first elected as the Vice-chairman of the Rajya Sabha in 2018.
- He is the first non-Congress leader to be elected to this post for 3 consecutive terms.
- He is the second person after Najma Heptulla to become Deputy Chairman 3 times.

question: Which North-Eastern state has granted official administrative recognition to Khasi and Garo languages?
detail:
- The Meghalaya State Cabinet has decided to grant Official Language status to the Khasi and Garo languages.
- The Meghalaya Cabinet approved the Meghalaya Official Languages Ordinance, 2026.
- This new ordinance will repeal the old Meghalaya State Language Act, 2005, in which English was the only official language.
- This will increase the use of local languages in Government Notifications and Administrative Functions.
- Now, Khasi and Garo languages will be used in Government Communication, Administration, and Exams.

question: Who has been awarded the ‘Para Archer of the Year 2025’?
detail:
- Sheetal Devi was awarded the ‘Para Archer of the Year 2025’.
- This honor was given by World Archery.
- This award was given to her for her historic performance at the 'World Para Archery Championships' held in Gwangju, South Korea, last year (2025).

**Sheetal Devi**
- Age - 19
- Birth - Jammu and Kashmir
- Arjuna Award - 2024
- Won Gold (Individual), Silver (Team), and Bronze (Mixed Team) at the World Para Archery Championships (Gwangju, South Korea).

**World Archery (World Archery Federation)**
- Formation - September 4, 1931
- Headquarters - Lausanne, Switzerland
- President - Greg Easton

question: Who has been extended as the Director General of the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) till March 30, 2027?
detail:
- The Cabinet Appointments Committee of the Central Government has granted a service extension to 1991 batch Uttar Pradesh cadre IPS officer Piyush Anand as the Director General of the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) until March 30, 2027.
- His tenure was scheduled to end on March 31, 2026.
- He took over as the head of NDRF after Atul Karwal retired in March 2024.

**National Disaster Response Force (NDRF)**
- It is a special force of India that works to save people's lives and evacuate them to safe places during any major disaster like floods, earthquakes, or hurricanes.
- Established - 2006 (under the Disaster Management Act, 2005).
- Headquarters - New Delhi.
- Ministry - Ministry of Home Affairs.

question: On April 1, 2026, which foundation year did the Reserve Bank of India celebrate?
detail:
- RBI celebrates its foundation day every year on April 1st.
- This time it will celebrate its 91st foundation day.
- RBI was established on April 1, 1935.

**RBI**
- It is the Central Bank of India.
- Establishment - April 1, 1935 (by the RBI Act 1934, on the recommendation of the Hilton Young Commission).
- Nationalization - January 1, 1949.
- Headquarters - Mumbai, Maharashtra.
- Ownership - Ministry of Finance, Government of India.
- Governor - Sanjay Malhotra.
- First Governor - Sir Osborne Smith (from April 1, 1935, to June 30, 1937).
- First Indian Governor and RBI's Third Governor - C.D. Deshmukh (from August 11, 1943, to June 30, 1949).

question: Who has been recently appointed as the new Director General of Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA)?
detail:
- Vir Vikram Yadav has been appointed as the new Director General of the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA).
- He replaced Faiz Ahmed Kidwai, who has now been made Additional Secretary in the Department of Personnel & Training (DOPT).
- Vir Vikram Yadav was previously the Additional Secretary in the Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change.
- He is a 1996-batch IAS officer of the Odisha cadre.

**Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA)**
- This is the apex institution regulating civil aviation in India.
- Its main function is to monitor aviation safety, pilot training rules, and airline operations.
- DGCA works under the Ministry of Civil Aviation.
- Headquarters - New Delhi.

question: Who has been elected as the new Prime Minister of Mongolia?
detail:
- Uchral Nyam-Osor was elected as the new Prime Minister of Mongolia.
- He became the country's third PM in 9 months.
- He has replaced Zandanshatar Gombojav, who resigned due to corruption allegations and legislative deadlock.
- Uchral was previously the Digital Development Minister and Parliament Speaker.
- He belongs to the Mongolian People's Party (MPP).

**Mongolia**
- Capital - Ulaanbaatar.

question: According to the latest report by the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), India has become which largest renewable energy market in the world in 2025?
detail:
**International Renewable Energy Agency**
- According to the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), India has become the third-largest renewable energy market in the world in the year 2025.
- By February 2026, India's total installed solar capacity reached 143.6 GW.
- India's total installed power capacity has crossed 500 GW.
- Out of this, the share of non-fossil fuel sources (like sunlight, wind, water) has now exceeded 51%.

question: Who has become the new President of Myanmar?
detail:
- Min Aung Hlaing was elected as the President of Myanmar in April 2026.
- Myanmar's Military Chief, Min Aung Hlaing, has been elected President by the country's pro-military parliament.
- He secured 429 votes out of the 584 votes cast in parliament.
- U Nyo Saw and Nan Ni Ni Aye, who contested against him, will now serve as Vice Presidents.
- Hlaing has been ruling the country since the 2021 coup, when he ousted the elected government of Aung San Suu Kyi.

question: Recently, Vice President C P Radhakrishnan released the translated version (Diglot Edition) of the Constitution of India in which language?
detail:
- C. P. Radhakrishnan released the Sindhi version of the Constitution at an event held in New Delhi.

**Significance**
- This is a Diglot Edition (in two languages – Hindi/Sindhi or English/Sindhi).
- This will make it easier for Sindhi-speaking people to understand their rights and duties.

**Data**
- The Sindhi language is included among the 22 languages of the 8th Schedule of the Constitution.
- It was added by the 21st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1967.

question: How many Gold Medals did the Indian women's team win at the Asian Boxing Championships 2026?
detail:
- On April 9, 2026, the finals of the women's category of the Asian Boxing Championships concluded in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.
- All 10 women boxers from India won medals (100% success rate) – this is a historic achievement.
- India ranked No. 1 in the women's category with 4 Gold, 2 Silver, and 4 Bronze (Total 10 Medals).
- Kazakhstan and China stood second with 2 gold medals each.

**Major Winners (Women)**
- Gold Medal Winners:
  - Meenakshi Hooda (48kg)
  - Preeti Pawar (54kg)
  - Priya Ghaghas (60kg)
  - Arundhati Choudhary (70kg)

**Major Winners (Men)**
- In the Men's Category, India won 1 Gold, 1 Silver, and 4 Bronze Medals.

**Overall, Medal Tally (Men’s and Women)**

| Rank | Country | Gold | Silver | Bronze | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Kazakhstan | 6 | 4 | 5 | 15 |
| 2 | India | 5 | 3 | 8 | 16 |
| 3 | Uzbekistan | 4 | 5 | 3 | 12 |

question: Who is the new President of Iraq?
detail:
- The Iraqi parliament elected Kurdish leader Nizar Amedi as the new president.
- He was the head of the political bureau of the Patriotic Union of Kurdistan (PUK) since 2024.
- Prior to this, he served as Iraq's Environment Minister from 2022 to 2024.

**Iraq**
- Capital – Baghdad.
- Currency – Iraqi Dinar.
- Its borders are shared with Turkey, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and Syria.

question: Which port has become India's largest Automobile Export Hub in April 2026?
detail:
- Mundra Port has become India's largest Automobile Export Hub.
- This port has created a new national record by exporting 6,008 cars in a single vessel (ship).
- Major markets for India's automobile exports include Africa, Latin America, the Middle East, and Europe.
- Mundra Port is operated by Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ).

**Mundra Port**
- It is located in Gujarat.
- It is India's largest 'Private Port' and largest commercial port.

question: Ladakh has been given an independent identity separate from Jammu and Kashmir by the UIDAI in April 2026. What is the new administrative code of Ladakh in terms of Aadhaar card?
detail:
- In April 2026, UIDAI made technological changes to the 'State Field' of Aadhaar, incorporating 'Ladakh' in place of 'Jammu & Kashmir'.
- This change has been made in accordance with the J&K Reorganisation Act, 2019.
- UIDAI updated the database utilizing its powers under the Aadhaar Act, 2016.
- Now, Ladakh has been assigned the new State Code 39.
- Meanwhile, the code for Jammu & Kashmir will remain 01 as before.

**New Administrative Change**
- Previously, Ladakh's Administrative Code was 01 (along with J&K).
- Now, a separate State Code 39 has been allocated to Ladakh.
- 'Ladakh' will now appear as an independent entity in Aadhaar records.

**Impact**
- 'Ladakh' will now be available as a separate option in KYC (Know Your Customer) and government forms.
- Tracking of DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) schemes will be more accurate.
- It will bring consistency to administrative and digital identity.

question: On 14 April 2026, the 135th birth anniversary of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was observed. In which year was he awarded the Bharat Ratna (posthumously)?
detail:
- The 135th birth anniversary of Dr. Bhimrao Ambedkar was observed on April 14, 2026.
- Dr. Bhimrao Ambedkar was awarded the 'Bharat Ratna' (posthumously) in 1990.

**Dr. B.R. Ambedkar**
- Birth - April 14, 1891, Mhow, Madhya Pradesh (then Bombay Presidency).
- 1947-1950 - Served as the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.
- For this contribution, he is called the Father of the Indian Constitution.
- He was the Law Minister in the first government of independent India (1947-1951).

question: Who has been elected as the new Prime Minister of Hungary?
detail:
- Tisza Party leader Peter Magyar has won the parliamentary elections in Hungary.
- His party achieved a two-thirds supermajority by winning 138 out of 199 seats.
- With this victory, he has ended the 16-year-long rule of Viktor Orban (Fidesz Party).

**Hungary**
- Capital - Budapest.

question: The central government has extended whose term as the chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission till April 2027?
detail:
- The Cabinet Appointments Committee of the Central Government has approved the service extension of Dr. Ajit Kumar Mohanty.
- Dr. Ajit Kumar Mohanty was first appointed as the Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission (AEC) and Director of the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) on May 3, 2023, and will now remain in this post until May 1, 2027.
- Note - Previously, he also served as the Director of the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) from March 2019 to September 2023.

**Atomic Energy Commission (AEC)**
- The Atomic Energy Commission is a government body in India that formulates policy and planning for activities related to atomic energy in the country.
- Establishment - August 3, 1948.
- Headquarters - Mumbai, Maharashtra.

question: Asia's 12 km long elevated wildlife corridor built under the Delhi-Dehradun Economic Corridor is located in which state?
detail:
- The most important part of the Delhi-Dehradun Economic Corridor is the 12 km long Elevated Wildlife Corridor.
- It passes through Rajaji National Park.
- After the commissioning of this entire corridor, the travel time between Delhi and Dehradun will be reduced from 6 hours to just 2.5 hours.
- This project is being developed by NHAI under the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH). Its total cost is approximately ₹13,000 Crore.

**Delhi-Dehradun Economic Corridor**
- Full Name - Delhi-Saharanpur-Dehradun Economic Corridor.
- Total Length - The total length of this entire corridor is approximately 210 km.
- Associated Park - Rajaji National Park, Uttarakhand.
- Unique Features - Underpasses and sound barriers have also been installed for wildlife so that animals are not disturbed by noise.

question: Which tennis player has been appointed as Global Brand Ambassador of Infosys in 2026?
detail:
- India's leading IT company Infosys has appointed Spanish tennis legend Carlos Alcaraz as its new Global Brand Ambassador.
- This is a multi-year partnership.

**Carlos Alcaraz**
- Country - Spain.
- Sport - Tennis.
- He is the youngest player in tennis history to reach the World No. 1 ranking in Men's Singles.
- He has won 7 Grand Slam titles so far.

question: 'Yana Caves has been declared a Geo- heritage Site of National Importance', in which state are the caves located?
detail:
- The Ministry of Mines has declared the Yana Caves of Karnataka as a 'Geo-heritage Site of National Importance' through the Geological Survey of India.
- This place is famous for its religious significance and the amazing black rocks found here.

**Yana Caves**
- Location - It is situated amidst the forests of the Western Ghats in the Uttara Kannada district of Karnataka.
- Geological Feature - It is a brilliant example of Karst Topography.
- There are huge black limestone mountains here.
- Two main rocks: Bhairaveshwara Shikhara and Mohini Shikhara.
- These rocks have been formed naturally over millions of years.
- There is also a temple of Lord Shiva inside the cave.

**Geological Survey of India**
- GSI's goal is to discover mineral resources in the country, conduct geological studies, and provide information related to natural disasters to ensure India's secure and sustainable development.
- Founded - 1851 (it is one of the oldest organizations in India).
- Headquarters - Kolkata.
- Ministry - Ministry of Mines.

question: Who has been named in the 'Young Global Leaders Class of 2026’?
detail:
- The current Chairman of the International Cricket Council, Jay Shah, has been included in the prestigious 'Young Global Leaders Class of 2026' by the World Economic Forum.
- He has been given this honor for the expansion of cricket at the global level, promoting women's cricket, and his administrative influence in the field of sports.
- This year's YGL list includes 118 influential leaders under the age of 40 from 55 countries, who are bringing change in their respective fields.

**Jay Shah**
- Currently, he is the Chairman of the ICC (he replaced Greg Barclay of New Zealand on December 1, 2024).
- He has been the youngest Secretary in the history of BCCI.

**World Economic Forum**
- It is an international NGO that brings politicians, business leaders, and intellectuals from around the world to a single platform to discuss global issues.
- Establishment - January 24, 1971.
- Headquarter - Cologny, Geneva (Switzerland).
